message:
fix(security)(ft-nrqf7): snapshot from_json — add size + depth DoS guards

TopologySnapshot::from_json had no input bounds: a crafted
.ft/snapshots/<session>.json could (a) blow the recorder's
memory by being arbitrarily large, or (b) blow the parser stack
via a deeply-nested PaneNode HSplit/VSplit tree. Result: DoS
on the recorder startup path (session_restore.rs:1258, :1493).

Guards landed:

- MAX_SNAPSHOT_BYTES = 64 MB. Byte cap fires *before* serde_json
  touches the input — attacker can't OOM by handing the recorder
  a 4 GB pane tree.
- MAX_PANE_TREE_DEPTH = 32. Caps PaneNode HSplit/VSplit nesting
  depth. Set to 32 (not the 64 the bead suggested) because each
  PaneNode level consumes ~4 JSON levels (tagged-enum object +
  children array + (f64, PaneNode) tuple + inner object), and
  serde_json's default 128-level recursion limit would fire
  first at depth 64. 32 leaves comfortable headroom while
  bricking pathological inputs (real-world fleets are <10 deep).

New TopologySnapshotError enum with TooLarge / TooDeep / Json
variants — distinguishes DoS guards from genuine parse errors so
callers can log and alert without panicking. Existing call sites
in session_restore.rs use `e.to_string()` and continue working
unchanged via the thiserror Display impl.

PaneNode::depth() walks iteratively with an explicit stack so a
pathologically deep tree cannot blow the program stack while
being measured.

7 fixture tests:
- from_json_rejects_oversize_input_with_too_large_error
- from_json_rejects_deeply_nested_input_with_too_deep_error
- from_json_accepts_input_at_depth_cap (boundary, must parse)
- from_json_round_trips_typical_capture (no regression)
- from_json_returns_json_variant_for_malformed_input
- from_json_does_not_panic_on_extreme_depth (512-level input)
- pane_node_depth_iterative_matches_recursive_intuition

All 86 session_topology:: tests pass under
--no-default-features (was 79, +7 ft-nrqf7).

Bead: ft-nrqf7 (P2 security-audit, snapshot DoS).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
